slots: Guide registration, trek briefing, dam views

Arrive at Bhira Patnus before 8 am — register with the forest desk and hire your mandatory local guide. Pack dry bags, two litres of water, and grippy shoes. Scan the notice board for flood or closure alerts before starting.
Stream crossings, backwater trail, basalt gorge

The 6.5 km path alternates between flat dam shoreline and shaded forest. Monsoon swells every crossing — follow your guide’s line exactly. The gorge narrows as you approach; you will hear the fall before you see the pool.
View the jade-green amphitheatre from approved vantage points only — swimming is banned due to depth and currents. Allow 45 minutes at the pool before the return trek while daylight holds.
Bhira village rest or Kolad drive
If legs are sore, enjoy a slow morning in Bhira. Otherwise drive toward Kolad along the Kundalika — the river that Devkund mythically feeds.
Ascend through Tamhini pass toward Pune — countless unnamed falls line the highway after rain. Stop only at safe pullouts and watch for landslide warnings on your phone.
Pause at Mulshi before dropping into Pune — the reservoir mirrors monsoon sky when wind is still.
When water levels permit (typically late monsoon into winter), book a morning rafting slot on the Kundalika — a natural extension of the Devkund watershed story.
Roll into Pune for a celebratory misal or bakarwadi stop — three days of Sahyadri forest complete.
Devkund access rules change yearly — verify forest department updates before planning a return monsoon visit.
